en: Basic difference between Primary Auxiliary Verbs and Model Auxiliary Verbs is that Primary Auxiliary Verbs can tell its complete meaning without any Main Verb.
Whereas Model Auxiliary Verbs can not tell its complete meaning without a Main Verb.
Primary Auxiliary Verbs can change with Subject and Tense. Whereas Model Auxiliary Verbs can not change with Subject and Tense.
For example, I am a teacher.
In this sentence, we have used the M form of B because in Present Tense, we always use M with I.
So, B became M because of Subject and Tense.
And the second sentence is He can teach.
In this sentence, we can see that we have used Can with He.
And if there was any other subject other than He, it would have been Can.
And we see that Can does not change because of any Tense.
So, this is the difference between Model Auxiliary Verbs and Primary Auxiliary Verbs.
